title: [ARCH] Evaluate async subagent execution model
description: Research and design how to run async subagents — parallel tool execution, background tasks, agent composition. Review what Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and others do. Balance SOTA features with proven effectiveness. Key: modular design so these compose well with agent loop, harness, system prompt. Depends on clean provider/agent architecture.

logs.msg: Basic sync subagent execution already exists (spawn_subagent tool, SubagentRegistry, run_subagent). This task is about evaluating async/parallel execution: running multiple subagents concurrently, background subagents, result aggregation. The foundation exists but parallel execution is not yet supported.

Rescoped after feature audit. Two concrete deliverables: (1) Parallel tool execution — when model returns multiple tool calls in one response, run them concurrently with join_all (Codex does this with FuturesOrdered). Speeds up every session. (2) Parallel subagent dispatch — spawn_subagent accepts multiple tasks, runs them all concurrently, returns all results. Foundation for RLM-style multi-agent work. Background/fire-and-forget excluded — too complex for TUI context. Sync execution exists; this task is about parallelism.

Parallel tool execution: already implemented via JoinSet in agent/tools.rs:execute_tools_parallel, called from agent/mod.rs. Parallel subagent dispatch: already works via the parallel tool execution — if model emits multiple spawn_subagent calls in one response, they run concurrently. Both deliverables complete. No new code needed.
